skill.name: low-frequency-thread-monitor
skill.description: 低频巡检 Skill for ordinary non-Goal Codex controller windows that need active, ETA-aware monitoring of background worker/review/planning threads. Use to keep the monitor alive until a terminal state, reduce context pollution and token spend, avoid over-polling, minimize expected wasted waiting time, and decide the next allowed action only after a final report, failure, or blocker appears.
skill.summary: ETA-aware background thread monitor for non-Goal Codex controller windows.
skill.author: ggsq003-png

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a pure documentation/protocol package: a 185-line SKILL.md with markdown instructions, a 147-line README.md, a 5-line agents/openai.yaml interface descriptor, and an MIT LICENSE. There is no executable code. Backticks flagged as 'shell backtick execution' are markdown inline-code spans (e.g. status templates like `低频巡检中，不是最终结论。` and timing formulas like `wasted_wait = next_monitor_read_time - worker_completion_time`). URLs flagged as 'hardcoded' are plain documentation links to skills.sh, github.com, and codexmonitor.app. The '~/.codex/skills/...' path is an install instruction in a fenced code block. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' matches are spurious triggers on natural language words like 'monitor', 'heartbeat', 'terminal', and 'review'. No network calls, no file operations, no process execution, no secrets, and no prompt-injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks flagged as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 42 instances of backticks in README.md and SKILL.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All are markdown inline-code spans around human-readable status templates (e.g. `低频巡检中，不是最终结论。`) and timing formulas (e.g. `wasted_wait = next_monitor_read_time - worker_completion_time`). No code interpreter or shell ever executes these strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by direct reading of the surrounding text: every flagged line is fenced or inline markdown, not Ruby or shell source. Highest confidence dismissal.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 9 URLs (skills.sh, github.com, codexmonitor.app) as network risk. They are plain markdown link targets in README.md, used as documentation references to related projects and the install command. The skill itself makes no network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs appear inside markdown link syntax [text](url) or fenced bash install blocks; there is no fetch/curl/HTTP client invocation anywhere in the package.

low_findings.title: Home-directory path in install instructions
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the '~/.codex/skills/...' path in README.md as 'hidden file access'. The path appears in two fenced bash install snippets (`git clone https://github.com/ggsq003-png/low-frequency-thread-monitor ~/.codex/skills/low-frequency-thread-monitor`) - a documented install location, not a runtime read/write of hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct read of the surrounding context confirms the path is inside a ```bash``` fenced code block used to instruct the user on where to clone the repo. The skill itself never touches the filesystem.

low_findings.title: Spurious 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' pattern matches
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 11 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' hits and 4 'system reconnaissance' hits on descriptive English words. Examples: README.md:40 contains the word 'Heartbeats' in a comparison table; SKILL.md:3 contains 'ETA-aware monitoring' in a description; SKILL.md:52 contains 'Terminal states' as a heading. None of these lines contain any cryptographic primitive, hashing, key handling, or reconnaissance logic. The matches appear to be over-broad lexical triggers on words like 'monitor', 'terminal', 'heartbeat', and 'review'.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection of every flagged line shows natural language only, with no hash/cipher/key/scan/recon primitives. The skill is pure instructional markdown; cryptography and reconnaissance are not in scope.

content.user_title: Monitor Codex Threads with Low-Frequency ETA Checks
content.value_statement: Non-Goal Codex controller windows can close too early or poll too often, wasting context and quota. This skill provides a low-cost, ETA-aware monitoring protocol that keeps the monitor alive until a terminal state appears.
actual_capabilities: Keeps the monitor window active until a worker thread reaches a terminal state
Applies ETA-aware next-check timing instead of fixed intervals
Avoids nudging or interrupting running workers via a non-interruption rule
Reads only recent status with bounded turn and output limits by default
Separates self-report, review, absorption, approval, and routing steps
Provides interim and terminal status message templates in English and Chinese
limitations: Monitoring only - does not authorize writes, approvals, closures, or gate changes
Requires a wakeup, timer, or continuation mechanism for the next check
Designed for ordinary non-Goal Codex windows and does not replace Goal mode
Behavior depends on the host environment keeping the monitor window active

use_cases.title: Controller + Worker Codex Workflows
use_cases.description: Run a controller window that supervises one or more background Codex worker windows without burning context on repeated polls.

use_cases.title: Long Review and Research Threads
use_cases.description: Supervise long-running reviews, research, or file audits with adaptive 8-15 minute windows and bounded turn reads.

use_cases.title: Control Hub and Knowledge Hub Routing
use_cases.description: Route worker outputs through Control Hub or Knowledge Hub while keeping candidate, active, display, and consume states separate.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Worker Monitor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor to watch this background Codex worker. Keep the monitor active until a final report, failure, or blocker appears. Do not interrupt the worker.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup of a single controller and worker pair

prompt_templates.title: ETA-Aware Long Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Monitor this long-running review with low-frequency-thread-monitor. Read only the latest 1-3 turns, schedule the next check in 8-15 minutes, and avoid interrupting the worker while it is reading or writing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Supervising a long file or source review

prompt_templates.title: P25/P50/P80 Adaptive Timing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor with P25/P50/P80 adaptive timing. Classify the task as long_review, schedule the next read near P80 or in 5 minutes whichever is sooner, and report interim status without final closure until a terminal state appears.

prompt_templates.title: Environment Without Wakeups
prompt_templates.prompt: Use low-frequency-thread-monitor in an environment with no wakeup or timer. State the limitation, name the exact next check time, and report the monitor as still active until a final state appears.
prompt_templates.scenario: Host environments that cannot keep the monitor window alive automatically

output_examples.input: Worker is still running, no final report yet.
output_examples.output: 低频巡检中，不是最终结论。 目标窗口还在执行。 下一次巡检：约 14:35。 当前动作：等待，不打断窗口。

output_examples.input: Worker reached a terminal state with a final report.
output_examples.output: 我低频读了一次窗口。当前状态是：已完成。 现在能确认：summary file at /path/to/final.md。 还不能确认：无。 下一步建议：路由到 Knowledge Hub。

output_examples.input: Worker self-reported progress but no final report.
output_examples.output: 低频巡检中，不是最终结论。 目标窗口还在执行。 下一次巡检：约 14:42（约 4 分钟后）。 当前动作：等待，不打断窗口。

best_practices: Classify the task first, then pick the next-check window from the timing table.
Prefer turnLimit 2 or 3 and includeOutputs false for routine reads; use includeOutputs true only for failure diagnosis or a small cited final output.
Treat worker self-report, review pass, absorption, approval, and routing as separate steps rather than merging them into a single approval.
anti_patterns: Closing or archiving the monitor just because no foreground text is being generated.
Using a fixed 'wait 5 minutes' rule regardless of elapsed time or latest status.
Sending 'continue', 'hurry', or 'are you done' messages to a running worker.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code or call external APIs?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation and protocol skill with markdown instructions, YAML metadata, and an MIT license. It performs no network, file, or process operations of its own.

faq.question: Is Goal mode required?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for ordinary non-Goal Codex controller windows. It complements Goal mode but does not replace it.

faq.question: How does the skill decide when to check the worker again?
faq.answer: It uses an adaptive timing table based on task class, elapsed time, latest status, and estimated remaining time. Near-finish states use 1-3 minute windows; long-running work uses 8-15 minutes.

faq.question: Will the skill nudge the worker for progress updates?
faq.answer: No. The non-interruption rule forbids messages like 'continue', 'hurry', or 'are you done' while the worker is running.

faq.question: What does a terminal state look like?
faq.answer: A final report matching the requested output, a clear completed status, a clear failure or blocker report, a handoff, or an explicit Owner-input request.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported and how do I install it?
faq.answer: Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install with `npx skills add ggsq003-png/low-frequency-thread-monitor -g -y` or clone the repo into your Codex skills folder.
